author: Victoria
comment_content: You need to really focus on the lectures to be able to understand. Of course, you already have the basics of calculus from high school, but trust me, once you see the mid term--it's really different. However, I enjoy learning math so this is actually one of my favorite course lol
comment_teaching: Professor is great and is willing to help out each student. After explaining the materials he usually gives a few exercises and looks around the class to make sure that the students are able to do the exercises. His power point is also very clear.
comment_grading:
Grading overall is a bit harsh, though.

Homework: 12%--easy, everyone usually gets 100% unless they missed the deadline
Midterm: 33%--tougher than the finals (I think), got around mean (actually 3 points below, but doesn't really matter as SD is around 15)
Final: 55% (relatively easier, and the mean is freaking high for maths. I got around mean+ ~0.7SD).

I got a B in the end.

This term's mean is really high compared to previous years', but judging from the past papers, our paper is relatively easier.
comment_workload: Workload is light but exams are pretty difficult.

author: venska
comment_content: There are 5 chapters on basic calculus, including functions, limits, differentiation, applications of differentiation, and basic integration.
comment_teaching: Teaching materials are taught in an organized and elaborate manner. The professor gave specific lecture notes that helps a lot for the course. He begins each class with a PRS question, which I think helps to make students aware of frequent mistakes. He will also spend time to go through your questions, especially during the paper check so that you know your mistakes.
comment_grading: The grading will be taken from Webwork (12%), 1 Midterm (33%), and Final (55%). The past papers might give you an image of the midterm and final.
comment_workload: Webwork every few weeks

comment_workload: There is only around ten webwork for the whole sem. You won't spend more than an hour to finish it. Super easy.

overall= +1.00 s.d.
A-

author: Spaceship

He takes rigorous definitions of mathematical terms quite seriously, which is helpful for "clearing concepts". Even so, skipping lectures is suggested because his teaching is best suited for students w/o calculus backgrounds.
comment_grading:
Reasonable grading (but not great of course).
Since students w/o calculus backgrounds can now enroll in math1003/1012 instead, the grading should be worse than before, especially when facing NDS (who almost always achieve top scores).
comment_workload: The workload is not that much tbh--only 9 webworks in total. Doing extra math problems before exams is recommended to avoid bad results.

author: 大积霸
title: 选择鄭錦恒作为您的教授
comment_content:
本人读的1013的L1。
要注意如果要读Math Major的要去选L1。

L1侧重证明，其他Lecture侧重计算。





课程概况
MATH1013 是港科大主流单变量微积分入门课，属于微积分 I+II 序列的第一门，面向有 DSE M1/M2 或同等数理基础的学生，以直观教学、重计算与应用为核心，为理工、商院等专业打数理基础。
核心内容
函数、反函数、三角函数与指数对数函数
极限、连续性、导数定义与求导法则
导数应用（极值、优化、洛必达、中值定理）
定积分、微积分基本定理、换元积分
课程优点
定位清晰，适配多数专业
不搞抽象证明，侧重计算与应用，完美匹配工院、商院、理院非纯数专业的后续课程需求。
教学体系成熟
课件、习题、辅导课（tutorial）配套完整，WeBWork 在线作业自动批改，学习路径清晰。
师资与答疑友好
多数讲师逻辑清晰、录课上传，论坛与邮件答疑及时，课后可一对一沟通问题。
难度适中，节奏可控
比 MATH1023（偏分析证明）简单，比 MATH1012 更扎实，适合想稳拿学分的学生。
课程不足
进度偏快
每周 3 小时 Lecture，知识点密度大，零基础容易跟不上。
考核偏应试
期末占比超 50%，题型灵活，临时抱佛脚很难拿高分。
习题量较大
WeBWork 作业 + 辅导课习题，需要持续投入，容易堆积任务。
评分与考核（常规版）
在线作业（WeBWork）：10%–15%
期中考试：35%
期末考试：50%–55%
无课堂参与分，考试决定最终成绩
学习建议
每周跟紧 Lecture，及时补录播，不堆积知识点。
认真完成 WeBWork，它是期中 / 期末的原题题库。
多刷往年真题，熟悉题型与计算套路。
辅导课积极提问，解决计算与概念漏洞。
适合人群
有 DSE M1/M2、A-Level Further Math、IB HL (AA) 基础的学生
工院、商院、理院非纯数专业，需要扎实微积分基础的同学
不想啃抽象证明、偏好计算 + 应用的学生
总结
MATH1013 是港科大性价比很高的主流微积分课，内容实用、难度友好、体系完善，只要保持每周投入，稳过甚至拿 A 不难，是大一数理必修课的优选。
要不要我帮你再写一版更简短的选课参考版，方便直接贴选课群？
comment_teaching:
为了Henry，我spring学期在没法Swap的情况下，直接翘掉L1去他教的L2了。
Henry对概念的讲解是真的有理解，用最简单的英文描述最复杂的概念。有时候听得有画面的时候我直接颅内膏超了。
有时候会有梗图放出，逗你一笑。

唯一的坏毛病是，时不时会在快下课的时候速教10页PPT，然后启动menti的扫码问答。使得经常我一脸懵逼然后带着疑惑下课。
comment_grading:
不建议去TA课，没什么TA教得很好。问豆包更快。


OVERALL 77
A-
拉curve
comment_workload: 作业略多略难，要用AI对答案。

author: Anonymous191818
title: Easy calculus course if you take M2 in HKDSE
comment_content:
A bit repetitive compare to dse m2. Here is the content.
1. limit.
2. Derivative+ curve sketching.
3. Integration.
4. Some random theorems(Actually this is the only new thing compare to m2) eg. Mean value theorem, Intermediate value theorem, Fundamental theorem of calculus.
comment_teaching: Teaching is okay but not necessary. I skip many lesson and still can follow.
comment_grading: Homework(10% of total grade) is on a platform called webwork and you have unlimited attempt to answer a question. Midterm(35% of total grade) is 1.5 hour long. Final(55% of total grade) is 3 hour long. For your reference, midterm 90/100+ final 96.5/100 result in A+
comment_workload: 0 workload in my opinion. You can use Wolfram alpha to get the answer for homework and do an assignment within 15 min.

author: cpcheung
comment_content:
For L1 section ,
chapter 0 : Preliminaries
chapter 1: Functions
chapter 2 : Limits
chapter 3 : Derivatives
chapter 4: Mean Value Theorem

comment_grading: For me , the grade is not too bad .But this course is not absolute grating . if you want to get b+ , total source must be at least 70%

Not particularly reasonable but still ok.
comment_workload: Overall workload is normal and can be handled. But do not study everything right before the finals week. 1-2 hours per homework due almost every 1.5 weeks. Read through tutorial notes then it will be fine.

comment_teaching:
Non-L1 review
==CHIANG, Yik Man== E
zzzzzzzzz he spent a whole lecture explaining what is set and other stuff. His tone is also really boring, which make students want to fall asleep

==CHENG, Kam Heng== A
Henry teaches WAY better than Chiang. If you have the fortune to be enrolled into his session, don't drop/swap it. He explain the ppt and notes clearly. He will also show how to do question in the lesson.

==YAN, Bokai (TA)== B-
Normal mainland TA, bit mainland accent but acceptable, will do some exercises in the lesson but not compulsory, so attend if you like la. Notes are really nice and great for deadline fighting before a final or midterm
edit: he played Doraemon in the last lesson, and there are calculus questions in that Doraemon episode
His creativity deserves a C -> B- adjustment.
comment_grading:
8 Homework: 25%
Final Exam: 75%
Unlimited Attempt of Homework so basically if you finish it on time, you could get 100% for homework.
Final Exam consists of three parts, 25 MC Questions in total.

Scored 100% for Homework, 84% for Final Exam. Final Score 88% and got an A-. Fair grading. Seems to be absolute grading so just try your best to study for your exams.

BTW unlike other courses, the scores are released immediately after Parts of the Exam.

FYI Got 5* in DSE Maths, 5 in M2
comment_workload:
Quite light. But depends on do you have calculus background.
Only 8 hw to do. About 20-25 problems per hw.
If you never learned calculus before, you might need a lot of help and find it really time-consuming.

Also there are many problem sets on classviva you can do (if you want to).

comment_grading: very horrible grading

author: Derrickliii
comment_content:
There's no attendance requirement. I only attended the first lecture.
I reviewed the syllabus once or twice, but that wasn't a big deal.
This book uses James Stweart's calculus textbook. It's thorough and includes numerous examples (and exercises). You don't need to read it all, and most of the exercises in the book exceed the exam requirements (if you're just looking for a good grade).
Overall, the difficulty level is moderate, making it suitable for beginners learning calculus.
comment_teaching: The instructor is not important; what is important is the large number of mainland students in the class.
comment_grading: Grading is also very individual. Some people think that the isolation sections, which mostly cover DSE, make it easier to pass the grade. However, for someone like me who regularly counts mistakes, Level 1 grading is much easier because it focuses on conceptual understanding, so careless mistakes result in fewer points deducted.
comment_workload: The study load was heavy, especially when taking other courses at the same time, such as PHYS1112. I felt overwhelmed at times because the combined demands of the two courses were too much to bear.

author: poorenginedog
title: this is easy
comment_content: The subjects covered encompass the applications of definite integrals, improper integrals, vectors, curves and parametric equations, modeling utilizing differential equations, solving basic differential equations, infinite sequences and series, as well as power series and Taylor series.
comment_teaching: The individual in question originates from mainland China and exhibits a pronounced accent, which poses a challenge in terms of comprehension. The teaching assistant, also from mainland China, primarily reads the solutions aloud and rewrites them, an approach that provides limited assistance. However, attendance is not mandatory for either the lectures or the tutorials. The schedule consists of two lectures and one tutorial per week.
comment_grading:
Grading scheme:
20% Assignments
40% Midterm (or 0%)
40% Final (or 75%)
Grading Scheme:
Homework (WeBWorK) 10%
Midterm Exam 35%
Final Exam 55%

85-100% ->A range
70-85% -->B range
50-70%-->C range
40-50% -->D
0-40% -->Fail
comment_workload:
Each week, you are required to complete one WebWork online homework assignment, comprising 10 to 18 questions. This initial assignment may require less time as it is intended to familiarize you with the platform and the types of questions you will encounter, with an estimated completion time of approximately 1 to 2 hours. Subsequent homework assignments typically demand 2 to 4 hours to complete. You are provided with unlimited attempts for each question, enabling you to learn from errors and enhance your comprehension. The objective is to attain full marks by reviewing incorrect responses and gaining a thorough understanding of the material prior to your next attempt.

The time required may vary depending on the complexity of the questions and your level of familiarity with the subject matter. The overall workload for each assignment is structured to be manageable, facilitating a balance with other responsibilities. The questions are designed to reinforce your understanding and offer ample practice opportunities. Your weekly WebWork assignments constitute an essential component of your learning process, fostering practice and mastery of the subject. By effectively managing your time and judiciously utilizing the unlimited attempts, you can optimize your performance and deepen your understanding of the material. Wishing you success.







2.1秒

The time may vary based on the complexity of the questions and your familiarity with the material.

author: Tieucam
title: Really challenging
comment_content: The content is quite extensive and diverse, mainly focusing on applications. The content is advanced from high school, not too difficult to understand but the exercises are diverse and the exams always know how to trick students. Before the midterm or final exam there will be some sample exams from 3 years ago. i found it not too difficult but when i took the real exam it was really shocking. after the exam my canvas exploded with comments from other students complaining about the length and difficulty of the exam.
comment_teaching: The professor teaches enthusiastically and always answers all questions.
comment_grading: This is a core course so it is very competitive, especially difficult for those who are not majoring in math.
comment_workload: There are not too many exercises, mostly in textbooks but you really need to study a lot yourself.

author: T.Yau
title: Very bad grade
comment_content:
Very difficult contents.
Mainly differentiation and little integration.
Before mid-term only simple limits calculation but there are many complicated application of d/in which is very difficult, especially to those who don't study M2 in HKDSE.
L6 rundown:
1 Sep 3, Sep 5 Basic set theory, numbers and intervals
Inequalities and absolute values
2 Sep 10, Sep 12 Functions and graphs
Elementary functions
3 Sep 17, Sep 19 Limits of a function
Rules of limits
4 Sep 24, Sep 26 Infinite limits and limit at infinity
Continuity
5 Oct 3 Derivatives
Rules of differentiation
6 Oct 8, Oct 10 Chain rule
Techniques of differentiation
7 Oct 15, Oct 17 Rates of change
Linear approximations, differentials
8 Oct 22, Oct 24 Newton’s method
Maximum and minimum of functions
9 Oct 29, Oct 31 Mean value theorem
ľHôpital’s rule
10 Nov 5, Nov 7 Curve sketching
Optimization
11 Nov 12, Nov 14 Antiderivatives
Riemann sums and integrals
12 Nov 19, Nov 21 The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
Substitution rule
13 Nov 26, Nov 28 Final review

No attendance requirement. I only attended the first tutorial as it is really rubbish. Went through the syllabus once or twice but it doesn't matter.
James Stweart Calculus is used. Detailed book with a huge amount of examples (and problem sets). Don't need to go through all of them, and most problem sets in the book exceed the requirement of exams (if you only want to get good grades).
Overall, appropriate difficulty to learn calculus from scratch.
comment_teaching:
This prof. teaches very good. The note is very fruitful with thorough elaborations.
Henry is a very very very very grood person, so nice, so friendly. He explains clearly af in lecture with steps
His notes are pretty clear and easy to read and understand
Also, his PowerPoint includes some memes for our generation to not to get bored while having lectures
comment_grading:
Very bad.

absolute grading
webwork 15%
midterm 35%
final (cumulative) 50%
對一個讀M1嘅人黎講依個course注定爛grade
comment_workload:
Not high.
Only about 1webwork per 2 weeks
No attendance taken, skip all lectures if you get 5*/5** in M2 shd be fine. Recording of lectures are also provided afterwards. M1 students might find it difficult but this is required course for SSCI or SENG so anyway. As PHYS1112 Professor Altman said, "Solve Problems"

author: JayLeung
comment_content:
Calculus. Half DSE-M2 stuff, half new things. Good luck if you are M1.
Here's a list(not complete) of non-M2 stuffs in this course:
Continuity, Differentiability, Intermediate Value Theorem, Mean Value Theorem, 'one-to-one' function, Solving problems with absolute functions, Sandwich(Squeeze) Theorem, Linear Approximation, Newton's Method, Critical Point, Rolle's Theorem, L'Hopital Rule, Reimann Sum, Fundamental Theorem of Calculus(A little more things than DSE M2)
comment_teaching:
Instructors don't matter, the number of monstrous mainland students in your lecture does.

I highly recommend that you:
1. Read James Stweart Calculus 7ed (this course covers part of the book).
2. Finish your homework ASAP, get extra problem sets if you're not confident in a specific chapter.
3. Get yourself prepared for the test by doing pastpaper.
4. MOST IMPORTANT: Know what you're doing. Know how and WHY you're carrying out the calculation in a particular way instead of another.
comment_grading:
As a M2 student and also in Spring semester (got many non local international students support me),
Got A-
But dont take this course in Spring course if you in Engin or Sci school becuase ir is the pre requisite of many thousands of courses and it will affect your timetable.
comment_workload: The workload was significant, especially when taking other courses like PHYS1112 simultaneously. It felt overwhelming at times, as the demands of both courses combined were more than manageable.

comment_workload: light workload; just revise what is taught in the class; do the homework and the past papers and you will be fine; also you can practice any extra problems in the canvas page and also that are taught in the tutorials....

author: BaoBao
comment_content: MATH 1013 is a very interesting courses.The course covered a wide range of topics, including differentiation, definite and indefinite integrals, techniques of integration (like substitution and integration by parts), and applications of integrals in real-world problems. The progression from basic concepts to more complex applications was well-structured.
comment_grading: I found the grading scale to be reasonable. The weight given to each component (homework, quizzes, exams) was balanced, ensuring that consistent effort throughout the course was rewarded.
comment_workload: The workload was challenging but not overwhelming. It encouraged consistent study habits and helped me stay engaged with the material. I felt that the effort I put in was reflected in my understanding and my final grade.

author: Zhang Jiahao
comment_content:
据说学的是IB数学内容，之前ib主要学了微积分复数向量矩阵这些内容
1. 微积分（Calculus）：
各种函数求导，这部分内容要求理解偏导数、梯度等概念。
微分方程：学习一阶和二阶微分方程的解法以及实际应用。微分方程在建模、物理应用中很常见，但解法复杂，理解难度大。
积分应用复数（Complex Numbers）：复平面（Argand Plane）：理解复数的几何表示，包括极坐标形式、共轭复数等。复数的根（Roots of Complex Numbers）：涉及复数的幂运算、开方以及解复数方程，尤其是德莫弗公式的应用。复变函数好像没学。统计与概率概率分布（Probability Distributions）：包括正态分布、二项分布、泊松分布等，要求理解并能够运用这些分布解决实际问题。贝叶斯定理
其他就是向量立体几何什么，不涉及到较深入的线性代数。
虽然涉及到这些内容，但教的不深，初步有个概念就是。

comment_grading: 还可以，学明白了难度不高。平常也会占点分，就是平均的课程评分难度。

author: econphd
comment_content: During class, there are no PowerPoint presentations; instead, the instructor writes everything by hand. Therefore, it is advisable to take notes during class for better review later on. The course starts with relatively simple content, but it becomes more challenging after the midterm.
comment_teaching: The teacher is quite dedicated in their teaching, although it can be a bit hypnotic. The teacher has a high level of English and is a very kind and lovely person, always wearing a smile. They often amuse themselves during class and have a lot of patience. They also provide detailed answers to students' questions after class.
comment_grading: 這門課對於學生而言是相當友善的，作業方面有無數次的機會，不必擔心一開始就做錯。在期中考試和期末考試中，重要的是過程，不必擔心結果錯誤會導致全部扣分。此外，在批改作業方面也有商量的餘地。總體而言，該老師非常友善。
comment_workload: The workload is generally light, with the only notable task being to complete homework assignments early to avoid missing deadlines. Prior to midterms and finals, it is advisable to review sample materials. Apart from that, there is minimal workload. Overall, it can be said that the workload is manageable, and the professor is kind, making it a favorable course for students.

author: M&A
comment_content: Mostly covered in HKDSE M2, but pay more attention to some concepts like limits, fundamental theorem of calculus and Riemann sum. The content of this course might be a bit hard for M1 students, especially for implicit differentiation, trigonometric identities, as well as the differentiation and integration of trigonometric functions.
comment_teaching:
I attended all the lessons in real-time before the mid-term exam. After the mid-term exam, I skipped most of the lessons. Besides, I skipped most of the tutorials. However, I did watch the lecture/tutorial videos on the same day as they were conducted to make sure that I would be able to catch up with the progress.
The content he mentioned during lectures were easy in general, but he spent so much time explaining those easy concepts and I felt like he made simple things complicated most of the time, which might confuse students. Despite this, he was nice and willing to answer students’ questions.
comment_grading:
Assignments: 10%
Mid-term: 35%
Final exam: 55%

You got unlimited attempts for the assignments so make sure you spare enough time to finish them and get all of them correct.
You only have to finish 10 mc questions within one hour for the mid-term exam. The most challenging part would be the concept of limits, so you may want to put more effort in this topic.
For the final exam, you have to finish 18 mc questions and 3 long questions within 3 hours. Each mc question worths 3 marks while each long question worths around 14-16 marks.
People say that the grade of this course is determined on an absolute basis but I asked my professor about the grading mechanism and he said our grades were determined on a relative basis. Anyway, work hard to secure a satisfactory grade.
For your reference, I got full marks in all assignments and the mid-term exam. However, I screwed up in the final exam and I only got 86/100. My overall score was 92.3/100 and I got an A at last. I knew that if I had not lost any mark in the 7-mark question about Riemann sum in the final exam, I must have got an A+. But yeah this might be a punishment for my disrespectful act of skipping lectures and tutorials, and that’s why I have to pay the price. Therefore, do make sure that you understand every concept before you sit for the exams.
comment_workload: The workload is light. There were a total of 8 assignments and you get plenty of time to finish each of them. However, beware of the deadlines as the professor/TA wouldn’t tell you when the deadlines were.

author: Ahzzzz
title: Just Don't Screw Up Your Midterm
comment_content:
From the perspective of a DSE M1 student:

Actually when compare with M2 students, the major disadvantage of M1 students involve in limit. Limit is the most important part for midterm and M2 students are more exposed to it. For trigonometry, though it is a new topic for us, there are not much questions involve in complicated trigonometry during midterm and final. Just understand and recite the basic of trigonometry then you should be fine. After the midterm, the difference between a M1 students and M2 students is limited.

If you will like to have more practices on the content, e-book with questions and answers should be provided in the Classviva system. HOWEVER, IF YOU WILL LIKE TO EXCEL IN EXAM, doing past papers is important. If you cannot find answers for the past papers, you can do them with your friends and cross-check with them.
comment_teaching:
If you are registered to her course, I wish you good luck and highly suggest you to go for other professor's lectures if you will like to have a teacher to guide you through.
Although most of the professors are just PowerPoint readers, she is the more outstanding one. She has such a strong accent that you can barely understand what she is saying and she will sigh every 30 seconds during the lecture. At the finally lesson, no more than 20 people (120 in total) are attending her class.

grade: B-

author: ypl

1 Number and intervals, inequalities and absolutes, functions and graphs, composite functions, exponential functions
2 Trigonometric functions, inverse functions, logarithmic functions, inverse trigonometric functions

8 Linear approximations and differentials, maximum and minimum values.
9 Mean Value Theorem, derivatives and graphs, L’Hôpital’s rule.
10 Curve sketching, optimization problems, Newton’s Method
11 Antiderivatives, areas and distances
12 Definite integrals, the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus.
13 Applications of integrals, the substitution rule

author: jath
comment_content:
As an ex-M2 student in secondary school, plus I am good at maths (5 in compulsory part, 5* in M2 in the DSE), the content is extremely easy.

Topics:
1. Function definitions, sets, domains, basic function types, inverse functions
2. Limit easy, advanced definitions, limit evaluation, intermediate value theorem
3. First Principles, Differentiation rules (includes product rule, quotient rule, chain rule), differentiation of inverse functions, implicit differentiation, applications of differentiation (finding rates using chain rule, first derivative test for finding extrema)
4. Linear approximation, Newton's Method
5. Local, Global Extrema and relevant theorems (including extreme value theorem), critical points, mean value theorem, tonicity (including monotonicity), concavity, first and second derivative tests, curve sketching
6. Riemann Sums (left, right, midpoint), definition of definite integral, fundamental theorem of calculus, indefinite integration definition, area between two curves, integration methods (substitution. including trigonometric substitution)

If you have studied M2, this course would be easy for you as most content is covered in M2 (except for some topics like Newton's method), but in a more in depth mode, including some theorem names e.g. extreme value theorem. If your maths is good, even better. But if your maths sucks and/or you haven't studied M2 and M1, you should think twice before taking this course as the topics covered in one to two years in M2 shrink to a semester in this course, so expect things to be quick.
comment_teaching:
Ok.

For the lectures, Professor Chang taught things quite clearly and the pace was not too fast. Lecture ipad drawings are released after the lecture. He has a slight mainland accent but I was still able to hear clearly what he meant (but if you so fear accents, you can choose another instructor). His teaching was good. He was willing to answer different questions.

For the tutorials, I attended those held by Mengjie Fang. content in the lectures was repeated, but taught in much less detail. Examples were the main focus of tutorials. Sometimes after the due dates of the homework, she would explain the homework solutions. The tutorials are a bit boring if you are good at maths. So if you attend them, focus on the most useful parts: 1. practicing questions provided, and 2. ask questions about things you don't understand (although most of the times I didn't have to)
comment_grading:
Homework 25%
Final Exam 75%

Homework can be repeatedly done for infinitely many times, and each time you have finished, following is a report of which answers you have correctly/wrongly answered. So you should repeat each piece until you get full mark.

For the final exam, it is easy if you are so good at maths. If you are not as bright, you can do past papers (get them from seniors). Analyze the commonly asked topics and train yourself until you can master them (e.g. evaluating a limit by turning it into a definite integral, find the value of a constant such that two curves can be connected at a point and combine into one continuous/differentiable curve)

I missed one homework and scored full marks for all other pieces of homework, and in the final exam (all mc), I did one question wrong out of about thirty to forty questions. Final grade: A

I should not have missed that piece of homework.
comment_workload: Light workload. One or two pieces of homework a month, each to be completed in one or two hours if you are good at maths. Of course it would be a different story for those who suck at maths. No midterm, no quizzes, so the only time you would need to do revision is before the final exam.

author: Harshna
title: It's a pretty easy and basic math, I'd say a level over what you've learned in high school. If you pay attention in class and do the practice exercises you should be goof
comment_content: The content is pretty straight forward, you have Linear equations for the first semester. Linear equations include matrices, annuity, compound interest and simple interest. There are no attendance requirements so you can listen to the recordings whenever you please to. He makes use of both his Ipad and power point slides to teach. The Ipad is really useful as he draws quite useful diagrams through it. He is a little too slow, which is a good thing since then you have time to soak in information.
comment_teaching: The teacher is really good. He is super calm with his teaching and makes sure that his concepts are clearly explained. You can freely ask him doubts without being worried about his reaction. If you're stuck anywhere you can also take help from the WSC which has got average staff but they're good enough to clear up your doubts. His classes are not so engaging. He uses power point slides to teach which you get on canvas too. They are pretty useful when trying to revise concepts.
comment_grading: The grading system is simple. You are graded on our homework and you test scores. The homework can get a little tricky but nothing the MSC can't help you with. He gives a pretty reasonable deadline (around 10 days per home work). You have unlimited tries so it's easy to score a 100% on them.
comment_workload: The workload is not so much. The only workload you have is the homework, which isn't a huge thing too considering the time you're given to do it. He gives you some suggested questions to solve from the book but aren't compulsory to solve. I suggest you do those because the more the practice the better it is.

author: jz0149
title: I'm stupid this is supposed to be MATH1014
comment_content:
Arc length, surface area, integration by disk, integration with shells, should be a natural extension to things in 1013

Improper integrals and other integration techniques, ie. how to integrate sqrt(x^2 +5) dx (hint: use trig identities)

Polar coordinates and integration

Infinite sequences and series, sums of said series, divergence and convergence tests (HIGHLY recommend you study up on these and know which test to apply)

Maclaurin and Taylor series for functions (recommend writing the polynomial, trig and log functions you are used to seeing in Taylor series form as practice)

Ends with a short section on vectors - understanding cross and dot product, projections, and distances from points to plane, etc.
comment_teaching: Not bad but not great. Would recommend studying notes before class, and trying to make connections between concepts in lectures.

15% webwork
35% midterm
50% final
(don't remember exactly.)

My grade was 0.5SD above mean, final 0.7-0.8SD above mean, total A-. (don't remember exactly)
A range shouldn't be hard, but the final was relatively easy (mean of 70 something.) If you are well prepared, you should not be stressed for time, so avoid making silly mistakes, especially on the MCQ sections which are full mark or no mark (this is also true for MATH1013 as well.) Most of my mistakes came from the MCQ section in both midterm and final.
comment_workload: Strong foundation in MATH1013 helps. Highly recommend doing past papers and practice problems to find common threads between problems. Skipping lectures ok but the workload can pile on quickly, so if you have anything you don't understand be sure to catch up quickly.

comment_content:
Calculus. Half DSE-M2 stuff, half new things. Good luck if you are M1.
Here's a list(not complete) of non-M2 stuffs in this course:
Continuity, Differentiability, Intermediate Value Theorem, Mean Value Theorem, 'one-to-one' function, Solving problems with absolute functions, Sandwich(Squeeze) Theorem, Linear Approximation, Newton's Method, Critical Point, Rolle's Theorem, L'Hopital Rule, Reimann Sum, Fundamental Theorem of Calculus(A little more things than DSE M2)
comment_teaching:
Dr. Lam's English is clear, and he is willing to answer student's questions. However, his writes on the whiteboard in a less organised way, so it's not easy to follow. The teaching pace is slow, and he put too much time on explaining the fundamental knowledge that both M1 and M2 and even DSE Maths core should have covered, especially functions... He should have put more efforts on new topics such as L'Hopital Rule and more...

Mr. Kong's tutorial is better, at least he can demonstrate the examples clearly and remind us some important steps/tips in solving problems.

I think attending tutorial is more important than attending lecture. I skip most lectures that teaches M2 stuff.
comment_grading:
Semi-absolute grading. Much fairer than previous semesters.
15% WebWork 35% Mid-term 50% Final
Total score
85-100%-->A range
70-85%-->B range
50-70%-->C range
40-50%-->D
0-40%-->Fail
comment_workload:
Homework: Basically 1 WebWork(Online homework) per week, each consists of 10-18 questions. Apart from the first one you will need 2-4 hours to finish one, not very heavy workload. You can attempt questions infinitely many times , so try your best to get full marks in them!
No attendance required for any lecture and tutorials
Exam: Do your best in the relatively simple mid-term to secure a better grade earlier. All contents will appear in final exam, so don't forget to practice on evaluating limits for the final. I focused too much on integration and didn't do a single question of limits before the final, so I lose at least 9 marks on some relatively simple limit MCs, sigh~~
By Alpha703 on Dec 25, 2016 13 of 13 people found this helpful. 0

author: ZhuKexin
title: easy content, hard to be perfect and get A+
comment_content:
Course descriptionThis is an introductory course in one-variable calculus, the first in the Calculus I and II sequence, designed for students that have taken HKDSE Mathematics Extended Module M1/M2. Topics include functionsand  their limits, continuity, derivativesand  rules  of  differentiation, applications of derivatives, and basic integral calculus.Credit points:3Prerequisite:Level 3 or abovein HKDSE Mathematics Extended Module M1/M2Exclusions:AL Pure Mathematics or Applied Mathematics, MATH1003, MATH1012, MATH1014, MATH1018,  MATH1020, MATH1023, MATH1024, any MATH course at or above 100-/2000-level5.Intended learning outcomesUpon successful completion of this course, students should be able to1.Develop basic computational skills in calculus.2.Express quantitative relationships using the language of functions.3.Apply the concepts and methods of calculus in modeling and problem solving.

comment_teaching:
I skip all his class and decide to learn by myself.
I think he is a nice teacher but not very good at teaching.
comment_grading:
got full marks in miderm.
but didn't do well in final.

almost A+ but actually A

students from mainland should be able to get above A. but still need some efforts
comment_workload: light. but you should make sure that your homework get full marks.

author: Yu012120
comment_content: M1 M2 stuff mostly M2 though
comment_teaching: Great. Swapped to this class at add/drop and found that he is way better at explaining the concepts than the other professor that i attended.
comment_grading: Absolute grading. I got 5* in DSE M1 and A- in this math course. My final score is 86.xx It seems to have a better grade for M2 student given that the content is mostly M2 stuff. According to professor Lam, the cutoff score is not integer as stated and getting 90 would be an A.
comment_workload: I would say it is moderate as it is M1 student can followed up without much difficulty much less M2 student.

author: Kit9973
comment_content:
Week 01:
1.1 Review of functions,
1.2 Representing functions,
1.3 Inverse, exponential and logarithmic functions
Week 02:
1.4 Trigonometric functions and their inverses,
2.1 The idea of limits,
2.2 Definitions of limits
Week 03:
2.3 Techniques for computing limits,
2.4 Infinite limits,
2.5 Limits at infinity, 2.6 Continuity
Week 04:
3.1 Introducing the derivatives,
3.2 Rules of differentiation
Week 05:
3.2 Rules of differentiation,
3.3 The product and quotient rules,
3.4 Derivatives of trigonometric functions,
3.5 Derivatives as rates of change,
3.6 The chain rule
Week 06:
3.7 Implicit differentiation,
3.8 Derivatives of logarithmic and exponential functions,
3.9 Derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions
Week 07:
3.10 Related rates,
4.1 Maxima and minima
Week 08:
4.2 What derivatives tell us,
4.3 Graphing functions,
4.4 Optimization problems
Week 09:
4.5 Linear approximation and differentials,
4.6 Mean value theorem,
4.7 L'Hopital's rule
Week 10:
4.8 Newton's method (*),
4.9 Antiderivatives
Week 11:
5.1 Approximating areas under curves,
5.2 Definite integrals
Week 12:
5.3 Fundamental theorem of calculus,
5.4 Working with integrals
Week 13:
5.5 Substitution rule
comment_teaching: Actually, I did not attend all lecture and tutorials.
comment_grading:
In midterm and final, about 5%   mark is about prove, mostly is computation. If you deeply understand the content of this course, getting A-range is not difficult, but A+ need more careful than your knowledge.
my result: overall 92/100->A
comment_workload: it is not heavy. almost 0.5-1 hr to complete each webwork for me.

author: heremandy
title: MATH 1013
comment_content:
80% HKDSE M2 content

Week one: Thursday (Sep 1)
• Numbers, inequalities and absolute values (Appendix A)
• Coordinate Geometry and Lines
• Basic functions and graphs, composite functions (1.1, 1.2, 1.3)
• Exponential functions (1.5)
Week two: Monday (Sep 12) (Holidays: Sep 16)
• Trigonometric functions (Appendix D)
• Inverse functions, logarithmic functions and inverse trigonometric functions (1.6, Appendix D)
Week three: Monday (Sep 19)
• Tangent and velocity (2.1)
• The limit of a function, limit laws (2.2, 2.3)
Week four: Monday (Sep 26)
• Continuity (2.5)
• Limits at infinity and horizontal asymptotes (2.6)
• Derivatives and rates of change (2.7)
Week five: Monday (Oct 3) 
• Basic derivatives (2.8, 3.1)
• Product and quotient rules (3.2)
• Derivatives of trigonometic functions (3.3)
• Chain rule (3.4)
Week six: Monday (Oct 10) (Holidays Oct 10)
• Implicit differentiation, derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions and logarithmic functions (3.5, 3.6)
Week seven: Monday (Oct 17)
• Rates of change problems (3.7, 3.8)
• Related Rates. (3.9)
Midterm Exam: 10:15-11:45, Oct 23 (Sunday)
Week eight: Monday (Oct 24)
• Linear approximations and differentials (3.10)
• Newton's method (4.8)
• Maximum and minimum values (4.1)
Week nine: Monday (Oct 31)
• Mean Value Theorem (4.2)
• Derivatives and the shape of a graph (4.3)
• L'Hopital's rule (4.4)
Week ten: Monday (Nov 7)
• Curve sketching (4.5, 4.6)
• Optimization problems (4.7)
Week eleven: Monday (Nov 14)
• Anti-derivatives (4.9)
• Areas and distances (5.1)
• Definite integrals (5.2)
Week twelve: Monday (Nov 21)
• The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus (5.3)
• Indefinite integrals and net change (5.4)
Week thirteen: Monday (Nov 28)
• Substitution rule (5.5)
comment_teaching:
Professor is boring
The tutor is good and has a quiz with us.
We may visit the Math Support Center at Room 3011-3013 for help.
comment_grading:
Absolute grading.

Homework 15%                                                                   Midterm Exam 35%                                                                   Final Exam 50%                                       
       
Grade Scale
100% ≥ A+/A/A- ≥ 85%,
85% > B+/B/B- ≥ 70%,
70% > C+/C/C- ≥ 50%
50% > D ≥ 40%
40%> F

Getting a A- or above is easy for those who studied M2 in DSE
comment_workload: Only 1 webwork per week, very light workload

author: jouyangaa
comment_content: R 曬各tutor d practice嚟做先喺靚grade做法,個個tutor都喺悶.

author: kaminohokyu
comment_content: easy if hv study m2 in secondary school,only few things is new to you,but more question required answer include proof, but not all calculation
comment_teaching: poor teaching,usually cant listen well wt the teacher say.actually no need attend lesson because ppt can be found online.
comment_grading: grading is fair ,above mean makes me get b-
comment_workload: no workload,only midterm and final,but practise more really can make u a better grade.  Do pastpaper before exam is a must,because questions may be the same . source can be found on peter gao.

author: Cromvale
comment_content: Not very tough if you finished IB HL Maths. If one is good enough at studying to join HKUST, the content should not be very challenging. However, it is quite tough when it gets to MATH1014.
comment_teaching: Although the content is not very difficult, the teaching ability of the professor was not very good. Possibly because math concepts are hard to recognize at once due to its nature.
comment_grading: Quite average. If you get the average, you would probably end up getting C+ or B-.
comment_workload: Due to the weekly assignment, the workload is quite high

author: _ry
comment_content:
A bit different from HKDSE M2
Course Description:
1. functions and graphs
2. limits of functions and continuity
3. derivatives and their applications
4. basic indefinite and definite integrals


Some say that M1 didn't help much.
comment_teaching:
Lessons were absolutely boring.
She only went through notes once on classes without little further explanation. She talked very softly and pronunciation was a bit weird, I could hardly listen.
No attendance recorded
No iPRS
Tutorials were quite useful, and if you have any questions, you may also ask the tutor after class personally.
comment_grading:
Absolute grading:
85%+: A+/A/A-
70%+: B+/B/B-
50%+: C+/C/C-
40%+: D
40%-: F

Got a B+ (studied M2 in DSE)
comment_workload:
Assessment scheme:
homework: 15% (webwork)
Midterm 35%
Final 50%

For the webwork, you can actually visit math support center and tutors would always guide you to complete your assignment, so you can easily fully take the 15% in this way.

For exams, we had a midterm practice and final practice respectively, question types in exams were somehow the same as practice. If concepts are clear and you had studied the question types in practice papers, it would be fine.

author: Herman1031
comment_content:
Mainly about secondary syllabus with some extra topics.
Not so easy for those who didnt get a good result in m1/m2 in HKDSE
comment_teaching: Lam tsz kin is a good professor as he draws graphs and explains the concept very clearly
comment_grading: Absoulute grading implying that it is harder to get a better grade if you are not hardworking enough
comment_workload: Only webwork is required but it can be difficult for those who didn't study well in HKDSE since most of the topics in math 1013 are about secondary calculus. Better revise frequently to understand the concept

author: Angela Tsui
comment_content: Basic DSE M2 Stuff with little new stuff. Easy to understand if you attend the tutorials and read through all the ppt.
comment_teaching: Explained tonnes of examples during lesson, but not much in teaching solving skills. And also useless notes. Tutorials are good though.
comment_grading: Skipped all the lessons, taken DSE M2 before, didn't finish several WebWork. Mid-term and final exmaination >50%. Got a C at the end.
comment_workload: Almost weekly WebWork. Everyone have their unique WebWork question but they are similar, you can take reference from your peers' WebWork answer or you can google it. Most of the questions are 'google-able'.

author: hahayeah
comment_content: if you have studied M2 in secondary school and had got good grade in DSE, you are quite likely to be ensured that you can get at lease B range in math1013.If you are M1 student like me, you should pay more effort on attending the lessons and do more exercises. Don't skip the tutorials when you met a bad lecturer like mine.
comment_teaching: All in all, I do not recommend Dr Ye as a good choice as her mainland accent is a bit hard to be understood. But she won't count attendance so if you have confidence in your exam performance, just ignore her lectures and do exercises instead.
comment_grading: It is absolute grading for 2016-17 fall semester, I would like to recommend u to pay more effort on midterm so as to pull your grade up as the final exam will be much more difficult.
comment_workload: My lectures and tutorials do not count attendance and only webwork need you to pay effort on. It don't really have a heavy workload if you do not aim high on this course.

author: ZHUYUXIANG
comment_content: the content is quiet easy in my view and it just covers what you have learnt in high school. there is no need to spend more time on this for an engineering student .
comment_teaching: the instructor is quiet humorous. sometime he may  makes a funny mistake deliberately.  he always try to use some example which is familiar to us to introduce what he want to teach.
comment_grading: very good. A+ is easy.
comment_workload: workload is very very small, you just need to finish what is put on website.   for tutorial , there is no extra exercise but if you think you need more exercises, go to the math department.

author: Gerald W. Liu
title: Not so hard
comment_content:
Most of the content is taught in secondary school, so it's a very easy course. The materials are well-prepared, especially the tutorial notes. The textbook is not required at all.
Attendance is not required. In fact, I only attended two lectures (the first one and the last one) and the last two tutorials. If you have already learned the content before, the lectures won't help you a lot.
comment_teaching:
Judging by the two lectures I attended, I think the teaching was easy to understand. The professor is very patient with students. By the way, he always replied my email in two minutes, which is really nice!
The tutorials are known to be very helpful, so please attend them. The teaching assistant can answer a lot of questions after the tutorials. If you don't want to attend anyway, please print those tutorials notes by yourself and do the exercises, or you will deeply regret it in the exam.
comment_grading:
15% for Homework, 35% for Midterm, 50% for Final. It seems that you need 95% Total to get an A+.
Midterm mean: 72.59  SD: 16.94
Final mean: 63.91  SD: 15.49
Total mean: 71.39  SD: 13.51
100% ≥ A+/A/A- ≥ 85%, > B+/B/B- ≥ 70%, > C+/C/C- ≥ 50% > D ≥ 40% > F
comment_workload:
The workload is very light. There are 9 sets of homework on WeBWorK, each one takes about an hour on average. You have unlimited attempts, so it's easy to get 100% as long as you never give up. It is said that the Math Support Center at Room 3011-3013 is very helpful.
One more thing, no matter how easy you think the course is, please take it seriously. If you don't make any effort, your grades will definitely disappoint you.

author: Grace YUN
title: MATH 1003
comment_content: The content is with well structured and planned. Definitions and examples are well provided. Most of them are already taught in IB Math SL courses.
comment_teaching: She's fine in teaching, desctibing well in class. However, the contents that she's taching is just the copy of the PPT. In other words, her PPT is well structured.
comment_grading: Lots of students do already have a basic understanding in math. Thus it is hard to get a good grade.
comment_workload: About 8 or 9 webwork-quizes are given. Each webwork quize contains 10 questions. No other homeworks are requested.

author: CLL
comment_content: 最主要都係中學M2野，係多左o的theory同method
comment_teaching: 由於成日走堂，不便評論，但係個professor講英文有少少口音
comment_grading:
今年用absolute grade
85分或以上有A range
70-84分有B range，有friend 69.X都有B-，mean大約70，所以grade同以前比唔算差
comment_workload: 每個星期有一份webwork做，唔識可以去Math support center或者用online calculator都得，除此之外都冇乜野做，加上大部分內容都學過，workload算輕鬆

author: michael_teng
comment_content:
Content

The comtent covers the most important parts of the book. From corporation itself to its investing and financing. The class also includes a tutorial, whose schedule is decided in The first one or two weeks. My schedule totally conflicted with all its tutorials, even the later added one. So I have never been to one. But I think it's ok since the tutorial slides are very easy.


Teaching

Well, what you learnt in class is just NOT ENOUGH! You have no idea what is going to be in the exam! For example, the first part about corporation is covered too little, but it was highly required in the exam!



Grading

10% participation(9% PRS 1% bonus card)
15% homework 9out of 10
30% midterm
45%final
I scored about 87% in total and got a B＋... pretty disappointed. But as I said before, the class is full of experts, so you just can't expect a good grade.


Workload

A bit of heavy I think. The homework is just s

comment_content:

The exam paper seems to be a little bit easier than before

Topics include functions and their limits, continuity, derivatives and rules of differentiation, applications of derivatives, and basic integral calculus.
Better than nothing

This is an introductory course in one-variable calculus, the first in the Calculus I and II sequence, designed for students that have taken HKDSE Mathematics Extended Module M1/M2. This is an introductory course in one-variable calculus, the first in the Calculus I and II sequence, designed for students that have taken HKDSE Mathematics Extended Module M1/M2. Not much, remember to do all webworks

author: AnsonTing
title: Good course
comment_content: The content is a bit easy for university level. It covers a lot of high school knowledge.The principles are however very useful.
comment_teaching: The teaching is simple but easily understandable. The Professor is very nice. The class is very relaxing. In lectures, there is no attendance taken so you can go to the class freely.
comment_grading: Reading this class means you can easily get a high grade, since the content is easy and simple, and the lecture is also good and easily understandable.
comment_workload: There is nearly no assignments for this class.

author: Carl
comment_content:
Simple basic M2 stuff with deeper explanation. It won't be hard if you have studied M2 before.
1. Functions
2. Limit
3. Differentiation
4. Introduction to Integral
comment_teaching:
Frederick is a funny person. He makes jokes about the politics. Not boring at all.
He has his own notes provided which is easy to read and understand.
Interactive lectures with the use of iPad as "whiteboard" which makes the content easy to understand.
comment_grading: A range is not hard if you performed OK in both mid-term and final exam.
comment_workload: Webwork every week. Simple questions if you have listened to lectures.

author: JackyChung
comment_content: Most of them are covered in M2 in DSE. To get good grades, you need to utilise the questions provided in the textbook and most importantly, past papers.
comment_teaching: Lectures are basically useless and you have to go to tutorials to learn again and get exercises. You will see more question types in tutorial class.
comment_grading: Need to compete with science and engineering students, who in general have more mathematical intelligence. Business students can still get good grades if enough effort have been paid.
comment_workload: There are online assignments with unlimited trials (not sure if I remembered correctly). Mid-term and final exams could be difficult.

author: SL
comment_content:
The topics were not too difficult to understand as most of it are based on the HKDSE M2 sylllabus if you have studied M2.
The topics are:
limits, differentiation, applications of differentiation, and basic integration.
However, the questions that appear in the midterm and final are not as easy as the webwork or examples on the lecture notes.
comment_teaching: His English is not fluent enough, but ok for you to understand it. His explanation is very clear, but you may find his teaching a bit slow and the exmples in the lecture notes are too easy.
comment_grading:
The distribution is about like this:
Webwork 12%
Midterm 33%
Final 55%

The grading is quite poor because the mean is quite high and the grading is curved.
comment_workload:
Online assignemnt (WebWork) once a week
Midterm exam
Final exam

author: legendarychriz
title: Average course
comment_content:
This course is learning what you have learnt in secondary schools.
Excel in high school maths see this course as heaven but other see this course as a purgatory.
comment_teaching:
For Prof.Zhu, despite his accents he is able to explain the concepts in math, and will always encourage us to think and sometime he will call someone to show how to solve the math problem.
For Prof.Ku, his lessons are more boring, and so there were less people attending his course. He is just saying about his notes, ignore his speech and just go through his notes, you will be fine, since his notes are quite good.
comment_grading:
Mid term 33%
Final exam: 45%
WEBWORK: 12% (99.9% of student get a full mark since there are unlimited tries, and the amount is low)
BBA students always moan about the engineering students and science students have made the course difficult. I am an engnerring student and i find this offensive. I get beloe 1.5 sd in mid term ,which proves that BBA students can be better than engineering students, so don't blame engineering students and science students anymore! It's all about hard work ( and depends on what you get in DSE M2)!

M2 3: Work harder to get a B range
M2 2 or lower or M1 students: Good luck.

Final above mean ~0.5 sd
Still C+

Oh boy.

author: V.lylit
comment_content:
It's either M2 stuff (if you're in HKDSE) or basic math (if you learnt it before) or unknown stuff. I don't have anything else to say really.

PS M2, in full Maths (extended module 2), entails algebra and calculus.
comment_teaching:
A class of 100 or so ended up with only 10 or 20 people coming to class. I am very proud to be one of them who went to almost every lecture... I think that says enough about what other people thinks of the class.

As I took M2 before, I did not find the course extremely difficult.  What I found rather repelling is the instructor's horridly rude reaction when my friend asked him a question - "why don't you go get a M2 book?" WELL, if my friend took M2 before, would she have to ask you? (For that I decline to expose the instructor's name)

There are tutorials as well, but I only went to the first session because the classwork there seems to be quite easy (at least for me). I do recommend you go to the tutorials if you don't seem to get what your instructor said in class.
comment_grading:
Theoretically its grading is fair because you get score for how correct your answers are.  It becoems less fair-looking because of the math geniuses and all the people who took related math courses in high school.  
For a good grade, you need to have a strong calculus basis, carefulness (while taking the exam - duh), and some luck.  For strong calculus basis, either take related math courses in high school or get some help from your friends who did so.  No matter what though, you still have to do lots of practice.
comment_workload: You have a midterm, a final exam and some assignments on workweb (or a platform with similar name).  Workload could be huge if you don't have strong base and somehow have decided to tackle it on your own.  My advice is to get some friends to help you out, and then do lots of exercise <-- this increases the workload too...

author: GAO XINGJUN
comment_content: 课程内容设计全面，系统性地涵盖了函数、极限、导数、积分及其应用等核心模块。知识广度充分，确保了学生能接触到大一阶段必须掌握的关键概念与工具。在知识深度上，课程在保证教学大纲完整性的前提下，对部分主题进行了适当的引入，其更深层次的联系与应用有待学生在后续课程中自行探索。所采用的教材是国际公认的经典版本，内容详实，例题丰富，是课后自学和深入理解的优秀参考资料。课程内容理论性强，与实践应用的结合点多通过习题体现，具备良好的学术严谨性。
comment_teaching: 授课教师具备深厚的学科知识，对课程内容的掌握无可置疑。教学风格清晰严谨，讲课条理分明，逻辑推导过程完整。课堂节奏紧凑，信息密度较高，要求学生保持高度注意力。教学方式以传统的板书或幻灯片讲授为主，侧重于公式推导和例题讲解，确保了概念传递的准确性。师生互动频率符合大型基础课程的特点，教师鼓励学生在课间或办公时间提问，并能给予耐心的解答。助教团队在习题课和答疑环节中提供了有效的支持，成为巩固学习的重要补充。
comment_grading: 课程评分体系结构清晰、权重合理，全面评估了学生的学习过程与最终成果。成绩通常由平时作业、多次期中考试和一次综合性期末考试构成。平时作业促使学生及时复习和练习，是巩固知识的关键环节。期中考试有效地阶段性检验了学生的学习成效，难度与作业及课堂例题相匹配。期末考试覆盖全课程内容，综合性强，是对学生知识掌握程度和解决问题能力的终极考核。评分标准客观公正，侧重于解题步骤的完整性和最终答案的正确性，透明度高，成绩分布合理。
comment_workload: 本课程属于典型的高工作量基础课程，要求学生投入大量课外时间进行持续性的学习。每周需要完成规定数量的、具有一定挑战性的习题作业，这是消化课堂知识不可或缺的环节。此外，为跟上课程进度、预习新章节和定期复习笔记，需要学生养成良好的时间管理习惯。课程的整体难度主要来源于数学学科本身的抽象性和逻辑性，而非内容的冗杂。考试周期间的复习压力较为集中，需要对整个知识体系进行梳理和大量练习，工作量会显著增加。总体而言，所投入的时间与最终的知识收获是成正比的。

comment_grading: Nothing special

author: Ayu
comment_content:
Calculus. Half DSE-M2 stuff, half new things. Good luck if you are M1.
Here's a list(not complete) of non-M2 stuffs in this course:
Continuity, Differentiability, Intermediate Value Theorem, Mean Value Theorem, 'one-to-one' function, Solving problems with absolute functions, Sandwich(Squeeze) Theorem, Linear Approximation, Newton's Method, Critical Point, Rolle's Theorem, L'Hopital Rule, Reimann Sum, Fundamental Theorem of Calculus(A little more things than DSE M2)
comment_teaching:
Dr. Lam's English is clear, and he is willing to answer student's questions. However, his writes on the whiteboard in a less organised way, so it's not easy to follow. The teaching pace is slow, and he put too much time on explaining the fundamental knowledge that both M1 and M2 and even DSE Maths core should have covered, especially functions... He should have put more efforts on new topics such as L'Hopital Rule and more...

Mr. Kong's tutorial is better, at least he can demonstrate the examples clearly and remind us some important steps/tips in solving problems.

I think attending tutorial is more important than attending lecture. I skip most lectures that teaches M2 stuff.
comment_grading:
Semi-absolute grading. Much fairer than previous semesters.
15% WebWork 35% Mid-term 50% Final
Total score
85-100%-->A range
70-85%-->B range
50-70%-->C range
40-50%-->D
0-40%-->Fail
Me:
WebWork Full mark
Mid-term 90/100 (Mean: 73.43 SD:15.77)
Final 65/100 (Mean: 63.46 SD:15.37)
Total 79/100 (Mean: 71.89 SD:12.94) -->B
You can see that getting a mean will give you B-, much fairer than the past.
comment_workload:
Homework: Basically 1 WebWork(Online homework) per week, each consists of 10-18 questions. Apart from the first one you will need 2-4 hours to finish one, not very heavy workload. You can attempt questions infinitely many times , so try your best to get full marks in them!
No attendance required for any lecture and tutorials
Exam: Do your best in the relatively simple mid-term to secure a better grade earlier. All contents will appear in final exam, so don't forget to practice on evaluating limits for the final. I focused too much on integration and didn't do a single question of limits before the final, so I lose at least 9 marks on some relatively simple limit MCs, sigh~~

comment_workload:

需要注意的就是past paper还是非常重要的，建议mid和final之前都要尽量多做

author: khyipai
title: MATH 1013 review
comment_content:
DSE M2 stuff:
1 Number and intervals, inequalities and absolutes, functions and graphs, composite functions, exponential functions.

2 Trigonometric functions, inverse functions, logarithmic functions, inverse trigonometric functions.

3 Tangent and velocity, the limit of a function, limit laws.

4 Continuity, limits at infinity and horizontal asymptotes, derivatives and rates of change.

5 Basic derivatives, product and quotient rules, derivatives of trigonometric functions.

6 Chain rule, implicit differentiation, derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions and logarithmic functions.

7 Rates of change problems, related rates.

8 Linear approximations and differentials, maximum and minimum values.

9 Mean Value Theorem, derivatives and graphs, L’Hôpital’s rule.

10 Curve sketching, optimization problems, Newton’s Method.

11 Antiderivatives, areas and distances.

12 Definite integrals, the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus.

13 Applications of integrals, the substitution rule.

Content is very easy conceptually but getting good grades is hard, probably requires a lot of drilling.
comment_teaching:
Instructor was teaching high school introduction to math in first lecture, after that I skipped all lectures.
The lecture notes are not very useful. Better read the assigned textbook.
comment_grading:
WeBWorK Online Homework (10%)
Midterm Exam (35%)
Final Exam (55%)
I got C+. For your reference I had lvl 4 and lvl 2 in DSE math and M2.
comment_workload: Webwork asssignment not too hard, low workload

author: SamuelChansc
comment_content:
An extension of the DSE M2 syllabus.

1. Number and intervals, inequalities and absolutes, functions and graphs, composite functions, exponential functions.
2. Trigonometric functions, inverse functions, logarithmic functions, inverse trigonometric functions.
3. Tangent and velocity, the limit of a function, limit laws.
4. Continuity, limits at infinity and horizontal asymptotes, derivatives and rates of change.
5. Basic derivatives, product and quotient rules, derivatives of trigonometric functions.
6. Chain rule, implicit differentiation, derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions and logarithmic functions.
7. Rates of change problems, related rates.
8. Linear approximations and differentials, maximum and minimum values.
9. Mean Value Theorem, derivatives and graphs, L’Hôpital’s rule.
10. Curve sketching, optimization problems, Newton’s Method.
11. Antiderivatives, areas and distances.
12. Definite integrals, the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus.
13. Applications of integrals, the substitution rule.

TEACHING

Pretty decent teaching I'd say. He focuses on the concepts, ie showing us graphical representations and etc instead of computations, even though 1013 exams are mainly about calculations. However, one thing I find strange is that when he talks about the concepts, he often gives unfinished proofs because he says the full proof is too hard for us.

GRADING

10% Web Work
35% Midterm
55% Final
comment_teaching:
Good enough. My professor's note is excellent. You even don't need to go to the class just read her notes is enough.
Teaching is fair. Quite boring if you have already understood everything.
comment_grading:
Absolute grading:
85-100%-->A range
70-85%-->B range
50-70%-->C range
40-50%-->D
0-40%-->Fail
comment_workload:
Super light as the only homework is webwork which allows you to try unlimited times until you get the correct answer.
The workload is not heavy. You can finish those assignments quickly by using chatgpt or different mathematical software.

author: cheungkiki621
title: Ok
comment_content:
同m2差唔多，要好努力，唔好走堂。咁最後都會有好成績。學嘅內容同m2差唔多，Week 01: 1.1 Review of functions, 1.2 Representing functions, 1.3 Inverse, exponential and logarithmic functions

Week 02: 1.4 Trigonometric functions and their inverses, 2.1 The idea of limits, 2.2 Definitions of limits

Week 03: 2.3 Techniques for computing limits, 2.4 Infinite limits, 2.5 Limits at infinity, 2.6 Continuity

Week 04: 3.1 Introducing the derivatives, 3.2 Rules of differentiation

Week 05: 3.2 Rules of differentiation, 3.3 The product and quotient rules, 3.4 Derivatives of trigonometric functions, 3.5 Derivatives as rates of change, 3.6 The chain rule

Week 06: 3.7 Implicit differentiation, 3.8 Derivatives of logarithmic and exponential functions, 3.9 Derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions

Week 07: 3.10 Related rates, 4.1 Maxima and minima

Week 08: 4.2 What derivatives tell us, 4.3 Graphing functions, 4.4 Optimization problems

Week 09: 4.5 Linear approximation and differentials, 4.6 Mean value theorem, 4.7 L'Hopital's rule

Week 10: 4.8 Newton's method (*), 4.9 Antiderivatives

Week 11: 5.1 Approximating areas under curves, 5.2 Definite integrals

Week 12: 5.3 Fundamental theorem of calculus, 5.4 Working with integrals

Week 13: 5.5 Substitution rule, Review (if there is time available)

author: AlexT
comment_content: Half of it is the same as the concepts in HKDSE m2. Compared with m2, some more details of some taught in m2 were taught, but they are still quite simple and it should have no problem for students who learnt m2 well to understand the new parts added. Some completely new concepts like Newton method are taught. They are not hard and only occupy some of the course content. In general, the course is not hard for people who learnt m2 well.
comment_teaching:
English of the lecturer is not perfect but still acceptable and does not hinder my learning much. In general, lecturer presents logic, idea and concept clearly and always try to show the students pitfalls of confusing concepts.
The tutor is willing to answer student's query and very welcomed to student's questions. Despite this, the tutorial mainly is a session about her doing examples. I personally found it not very helpful and only a few attended the tutorial.
comment_grading:
10 percent for 8 web work hw
Because you can modify for unlimited times, everyone can score a 100 in this part
35 percent for midterm
Very easy only consist of 10 mc and 1 hour is given, and I consider it as very sufficient amount of time.
Though I missed 1 due to carelessness
And only scored 90 out of 100, the mean in this class is only 50 something. I personally think anyone should try to aim a 100 in midterm
Final accounts for 55 percent
19 mc and 3 long questions
Slightly harder than midterm
But still doable if you understand what was taught in lectures
The grade is not yet known but I expect a A range
comment_workload: Quite light if you have studied m2 well as more than half of the content is the same as m2. Only 8 simple web works which can be re submitted for unlimited times without any penalty to ensure 100 marks. In addition, sufficient time is given for each of these web works.

author: Kemist
title: Be careful
comment_content:
Mostly dse m2 stuff, if you did well in dse and don't want to study the on9 honour course, this is the best place to wash grade.
Course content(copied from another review lol)
1
Number and intervals, inequalities and absolutes, functions and graphs, composite functions, exponential functions.
2
Trigonometric functions, inverse functions, logarithmic functions, inverse trigonometric functions.
3
Tangent and velocity, the limit of a function, limit laws.
4
Continuity, limits at infinity and horizontal asymptotes, derivatives and rates of change.
5
Basic derivatives, product and quotient rules, derivatives of trigonometric functions.
6
Chain rule, implicit differentiation, derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions and logarithmic functions.
7
Rates of change problems, related rates.
8
Linear approximations and differentials, maximum and minimum values.
9
Mean Value Theorem, derivatives and graphs, L’Hôpital’s rule.
10
Curve sketching, optimization problems, Newton’s Method.
11
Antiderivatives, areas and distances.
12
Definite integrals, the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus.
13
Applications of integrals, the substitution rule.
comment_teaching: Prof Chang is from Taiwan, the accent is fair but not very good tho. I'd say the explanation is not bad, but one of the observation is that a 120 ppl lecture ended up having 40-50 ppl attending.
comment_grading:
Absolute grading
Total score
85-100%-->A range
70-85%-->B range
50-70%-->C range
40-50%-->D
0-40%-->Fail

25% hw (classviva)
75% final(all mcq)

Got full score in hw, 24/25 in final, total 97/100, A+
I got 5** in core maths and m2 in dse but don't want to study on9 1023 so came here wash grade. However, for normal local m1/2 students, the absolute grade is a nightmare. Be careful doing every mcq as one question=one grade.

Very light, no mt, only 8 online hw, due 1 month after releasing.
Try to ask for resources and do the past papers before the final exam and you'll be good. Ask questions in wtapp group if you need help. If you don't have one, form one.

author: rind
comment_content:
This course is designed for those students who have studied M1 or M2 in secondary school.
Here is just a brief syllabus:
Intervals and absolute value
Functions (odd, even, *inverse)
Limits
Trigonometry
Differentiation (first principle and equation of tangent)
Related rates
Graph plotting (asymptotes, critical points, point of inflexion, absolute max/min)
Newton's method
Mean Value Theorem
Intermediate Value theorem
Tangent approximation
Anti-derivative and definite Integral
Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
*L'hopital Rule
*Reimann Sum
Those topics with stars are those I think is quite difficult compare to the other topics.

As a M2 student, I think many of them have been taught in DSE syllabus except those I put a star on and the theorems. I do think the theorem are quite intuitive and easy to understand. So not a big problem.
comment_teaching:
I highly recommend Phyllis. The best tutor so far in UST course I have studied. In tutorial lessons, she will give us her notes and we will go through all the questions. Her notes is simple but extremely easy to understand. The most important thing is that these practices include the questions from past paper and she has arranged the question in a very smart way. After learning the theorem, you do the first question. Just apply the theorem is ok. However, the next question is usually sightly different from the previous one and most of us cannot finish it by just the same method. That's the trap students usually fall into when they are doing exam which makes the concept more clear. IN THE MIDTERM EXAM, AT LEAST HALF of the questions are similar to those in the tutorial notes. Phyllis is nice too and she can speak fluent English which hardly can be found in Math course. I have sent a email to her about her website is not working and she replied me within 30mins together with the soft copy of tutorial notes. I hope she teaches 1014...but seems she is not.

Prof. Lam is a very good professor in Math1013. Clear hand writing, fluent English and clear explanations make him the best. Highly recommend to sit his class. Remember to arrive the classroom earlier because you may not get a seat if you are too late.

LTK is teaching you to prove the theorems and definitions of them. Phyllis is to teach you how to apply them. One of the problems of Math1013 is that, if the theorem is proved in lectures. You should know how to use it but Professor won't teach you. So attending to Phyllis's tutorial is a must.
comment_grading:
Grading System:
15% homework (8 sets of homework, each 100)
35% midterm
50% final
Should be absolute grading

My scores:
Homework: 800/800
Midterm: 100/100
Final: 90/100
Overall: 95/100 A+

The midterm as I have said, most of the questions are similar with the tutorial notes (those MCQs that try to trap you) and past papers. Easy to get full mark if you pay attention to something basic.

The final change to online exam. Since it is open-book exam, they don't exam things like tangent approximation or Newton's method because you just simply substitute into the formula and things are done. Instead they examine something quite difficult, the exam is 2 hour long to finish 20 MC questions. The exam is proctored so suppose no one can cheat. The difficulty is extremely high compare to past exams. Mean is about 60.
comment_workload:
8 sets of homework, basically you have to spend 1-2 hours to finish it. You can have infinite number of attempts so make sure you get full marks for all homework.
Basically no workload since many of the syllabus has been taught in DSE.
No attendance for both tutorials and lectures, but I still attend LTK's lectures since there is still something important things being taught or prove in lessons.

There is no workload for me, if you have studied M2 in secondary school then Math1013 should not be a big problem.

title: Simple and Fundamental
comment_content: The content is basically designed for those students who will use calculus just as a tool in their own disciplines. So rigorous maths analyses are not emphasised. In other words, it let you know how to do physics/economics problems and is relatively simple.
comment_teaching: Dr. Lam can explain points clearly to students, with enough patience. But somehow, his pace is so slow that you may skip some of the classes (as long as you are confident of yourself). No attendance will be recorded towards the final grade.
comment_grading: I don't think there're problems getting into A range if you perform well in both mid-term and final exam. But the exams are really interesting that you may make some naive mistakes without noticing them.
comment_workload: Almost no workload, unless some simple problems on WebWork, and no deduction for wrong attempts. So you can finish them easily without any stress. I may say, the homework for the whole semester can be done in one day.

comment_content:
Fair Very Ver Fair

Calculus. Half DSE-M2 stuff, half new things. Good luck if you are M1.
Here's a list(not complete) of non-M2 stuffs in this course:
Continuity, Differentiability, Intermediate Value Theorem, Mean Value Theorem, 'one-to-one' function, Solving problems with absolute functions, Sandwich(Squeeze) Theorem, Linear Approximation, Newton's Method, Critical Point, Rolle's Theorem, L'Hopital Rule, Reimann Sum, Fundamental Theorem of Calculus(A little more things than DSE M2)
comment_teaching:
Good
Dr. Lam's English is clear, and he is willing to answer student's questions. However, his writes on the whiteboard in a less organised way, so it's not easy to follow. The teaching pace is slow, and he put too much time on explaining the fundamental knowledge that both M1 and M2 and even DSE Maths core should have covered, especially functions... He should have put more efforts on new topics such as L'Hopital Rule and more...

Mr. Kong's tutorial is better, at least he can demonstrate the examples clearly and remind us some important steps/tips in solving problems.

I think attending tutorial is more important than attending lecture. I skip most lectures that teaches M2 stuff.
comment_grading: Fair Very Very Fair
comment_workload:
Low

Homework: Basically 1 WebWork(Online homework) per week, each consists of 10-18 questions. Apart from the first one you will need 2-4 hours to finish one, not very heavy workload. You can attempt questions infinitely many times , so try your best to get full marks in them!
No attendance required for any lecture and tutorials
Exam: Do your best in the relatively simple mid-term to secure a better grade earlier. All contents will appear in final exam, so don't forget to practice on evaluating limits for the final. I focused too much on integration and didn't do a single question of limits before the final, so I lose at least 9 marks on some relatively simple limit MCs, sigh~~

author: Ethen
title: 龜好，但不易好龜 Good grading, but not so easy to get good grade
comment_content:
一些最基礎的函數和微積分知識

Some basic knowledge about functions and calculus
comment_teaching:
很不錯，語速夠慢，通俗易懂。Tutorial講的也很不錯。

Very good, slow enough to understand. Tutorial is also very good.
comment_grading:
本人來自中國大陸，經歷過中國高考，不想評價太多，如果你恰好也是這種，你只需要認真作對你會的題目就能拿到A+。

本學期情況是75%final（25題），25%assignment (8個）,
assignment全對的前提下，final錯一個A+，錯兩個A。


75%final（25questions），25%assignment（8）
When you get full mark assignment, you will get A+ if you make one question wrong in the final exam, and A if you make two.

This course will be really easy for students from mainland China, even if they skip lessons. Because most of the contents are taught  in their high school.
comment_workload:
非常輕鬆，assignment可以無限次提交，直至滿分

Easy to handle, you can redo your assignment indefinitely untill you get full mark.

author: ambroselin_
comment_content:
Basic calculus stuff. Easy to handle if you have studied calculus in high school.

Topics covered:

Functions and Graphs of functions
Transformation of Graphs
Limits
Continuity and Differentiability
Basic Differentiation (Trigo and its inverse etc) (Differentiate inverse functions)
Definite and Indefinite Integrals (Substitution rule) ,
Riemann sum  
Extreme Value Theorem, Mean Value Theorem, Intermediate Value Theorem,  Fermat's Theorem  etc
Linear Approximation
Newton's Method
comment_teaching: A very helpful professor who is really willing to answer student's questions. Explains concepts in great detail. Not really hard to catch up if you haven't studied M1 & M2.
comment_grading:
25% Assignment
75% Final exam

You can easily secure a full 25% in assignments. The mean for final exams is about 70%. 3 x 45 mins final exam sessions within 3 hrs with about 8 MCs each.
comment_workload: To be honest the workload is quite light. About 8x homework for the course, so about 1 in every two weeks. Quite easy to handle the homework and you can easily get 100% if you attend the tutorial sessions and do some basic revision. (shoutout to Yue Wu who is my tutorial session instructor, she is very helpful)

author: Henry0122
title: easy course
comment_content:
Topics of the course include:
number and intervals, inequalities and absolutes,
functions and graphs, graph transformations, composite functions,
exponential functions, inverse functions, logarithmic functions,
Trigonometric functions, inverse trigonometric functions.
Tangent and velocity, the limit of a function,
limit laws, continuity, limits at infinity and horizontal asymptotes,
derivatives and rates of change,
basic derivatives, product and quotient rules,
chain rule, derivatives of inverse functions, implicit differentiation,
rates of change and related rates,
linear approximations and differentials, Newton’s Method,
maximum and minimum values, Mean Value Theorem, derivatives and graphs,
L’Hôpital’s rule,
curve sketching, optimization problems.
antiderivatives, areas and distances,
definite integrals, the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus,
applications of definition integrals, the substitution rule.

Lam tsz kin is the best, other instructor is like piece of s

tutor is useless, tutorial as well, most of the stuff taught in tutorial will not appear in mt and final

i dun study a lot, cuz i hv studied m2 in dse.
for dse m2 student, miss all classes and learn on youtube is enough already
comment_grading:
Assessment Scheme
a. Examination duration: Midterm exam 1.5 hrs; final exam 3 hrs
b. Percentage of coursework, examination, etc.:
Assessment Assessing Course ILOs
15% by online homework 1, 2, 3
35% by midterm exam 1, 2, 3
50% by final exam 1, 2, 3
c. The grading is based on students’ performance in assessment tasks.
comment_workload:
to me, quite light workload, 13 hw in one sem.
only mt and final, no project, no quiz, no attendence, no presentation

happy course

author: 塵
comment_content:
简单的积分与微分
会从集合讲起，上课节奏很慢，大部分是高中学过的内容。
对于高考生来说，很容易衔接。
但是要注意有些的专有名词，且公式的表达方式可能会略有不同。
comment_teaching:
教授来自大陆，可以使用普通话为学生进行答疑，提高你的问题解决的效率。
教授很友好，如果考试的时候遇到不会的英文，可以举手提问，你会得到解答。
我没怎么上过课，不计考勤，自学即可。
comment_grading:
绝对分值，
85+是a range
midterm35%
final55%
hw10%

我的分数：
midterm 78/100（mean：68.71）
final 92/100（mean:77.36）
hw 100%
total:87.9/100(mean:76.11)
A-

建议做几套真题，刷刷手感。
comment_workload:
作业一共有九次。
时间间隔基本为一周。
作业的难度不是很大，但是依旧有一些题目有着一定的思维量，会应用一些特殊的解题技巧来完成，对知识的应用能力做一定的要求。
好消息是作业有着无限的尝试次数，基本可以做到满分，除非你没写。

4. Integral

comment_grading:

webwork 100

A grade
