one webwork containing 8-12 questions per week
those questions are not difficult, you can ask your friend for help or go to Math support center for help.
you can try many time as you like until you get 100%

author: uxgl7
title: easy course
comment_content:
Topics include functions and graphs, limits of functions and continuity,derivatives and their applications, basic indefinite  and definite integrals.
To be honest, I think the content is not quite difficult. For students who have strong interests in math, maybe MATH 1023 is more suitable for you.
comment_teaching: Professor Wang is a nice and kind instructor. Her pronunciation is clear and lecture rhythm is appropriate. What's more, I like her notes because they are well-structured which play a significant role in my midterm and final review.
comment_grading:
Homework 10%
Midterm 35% (10 MC questions)
final  50%  ( about 20 MC questions and 3 long questions)
My grade:
Homework: 100/100
Midterm:90/100
Final:100/100
Overall:96.5%  A+
comment_workload: The workload is not heavy. You have to finish 8 homework during the whole semester and each homework has about 15 questions.You can try to do it many times.The instructor only considers the highest score.

author: a.L
title: Normal
comment_content: All theory and concept, no practical things to be expected. Quite difficult if you didn't study M2 before
comment_teaching: online teaching is stupid, the professor just writes and continues, quite boring.
comment_grading: I hate online learning and I have no motivation at all. I just get a c+ with 50% in my final exam. Nevertheless, if I can attend the lecture on campus things would be different.
comment_workload: the workload is obviously basic since this is a number 1 course, it's not meant to be too challenging or demanding for students who first enter university

author: YuxinTang
comment_content: The course is not very difficult and teaches about/extrapolates from many concepts already learned in high school, but it still requires adequate time and exercises to achieve high like A+. Besides, some problems are interesting to work on. In a word, a inspiring(might be a bit boring for advanced learners) introductory course.
comment_teaching: Ms. Ye is a very nice teacher. She has mainland accent but it's generally easy to follow. She gives clear and useful summary of underlying ideas when solving problems. Also, her notes are concise and cover all the concepts and types of problems that'll tested, which are useful for building a basic framework. Though you still have to do some relatively more difficult problems from tutorials or past papers to pratice.        And as she explains all the steps to even simple problems very slowly to ensure that everybody understand, you can skip certain explanations based on your own need.
comment_grading: absolute grading for grade range: ≥85 A range but detailed grades like A+ will be determined by your position in all the students taking math1013 that term. Easy to get into A range but requires more effort to get A+
comment_workload: very small. Homeworks are all done on webwork and offer unlimited attempts to try different solutions. You only need  to get your final attempt right. 1midterm+1final

author: Kevin KAI
title: A GOOD GRADE COURSE FOR THOSE WHO HAVE STUDIED M2
comment_content:
The content is easy and not so difficult because most of them have all been taught by your M2 teacher in secondary school.
But there are some new things need to understand. If you aim to get an A or A+, you need to have a deepen understanding of those new things, otherwise you may lose some marks in final exam.
comment_teaching: The teacher is so good. His lessons are easy to follow. His notes are really good. If you want to learn more, he is really a good choice for you .
comment_grading:
GOOD GRADE because the grade doesn't depend on your competitors. This is absolute grade.
100% ≥ A+/A/A- ≥ 85%, > B+/B/B- ≥ 70%, > C+/C/C- ≥ 50% > D ≥ 40% > F
The grade depends on homework(15%)+midterm(35%)+final(50%), the grade depends mostly on your final exams. and it's easy to get 100 in homework because you can try again and again to get full marks.
But final exam should be more careful if you want to get a good good grade.
comment_workload: not so large workload. just homework and homework is not so difficult for you to do.

author: I Want 1st Hon!
title: Practises are important!
comment_content:
If you get star(s) in DSE M2 like me, you probably don't need to do any extra exercises before midterm. Just revise some examples and do the midterm pastpapers, then it is ok.
However, you need to do extra exercises/tutorials after midterm because nearly everything is new.

I just made serious careless mistake in final, 12 marks (4 MC) is deducted because of my carelessness...

I give "B" because it is just too easy for M2 students but I don't think it is fair for M1 students.
Also, many Non local students have learnt it in their high school, e.g. Function... Not fair for local DSE students.

I think non-local students should go to an advanced class since they have learnt most of the content in high school.
comment_teaching:
He writes his notes on a paper, and uses a visualiser to teach.

His accent is hardly understandable. A lot of people quit his lectures.

He will upload the materials he taught in class. I just read it by myself.

Tutors is very very great! Remember to register Leung Ho Ming and sit in the front! I will give him an "A".

But for Dr. Hu. I will definitely give him "D".

However, I appreciate his effort since I see him nearly everyday in LG3/LG4 Library and work hard on something, maybe teaching or researching.
Maybe he is a great great researcher, but NOT a good teacher. He is nice to answer your questions, even though you still don't understand what he is talking about (accent)...
comment_grading:
Quite fair. 85% above A range.

- Absolute grading
Midterm (35%): 92/100 (mean: 70.92, SD: 15-17);
Full mark in Webwork: 9/9 (15%)
(TOTALLY NOT USEFUL)
Final (50%): 81/100 (mean: 66.2, SD: 17.53)
Total Mark: 32.2+15+ 40.5 = 87.7
Grade: A- (3.7)
comment_workload:
Do exercises. Nothing else. Quite normal.
Not necessary to attend tutorials, but I suggest you attend all of Leung Ho Ming's tutorials if you have things that don't understand!

comment_content:
以前學m2係不停操題目，講兩句concept就開始做，呢個course更加著重概念，內容就唔係難，但最難既一樣野係改變以前既學習模式，從學做題目到學概念 e.g limit definition of differentiation同riemann sum之類

本身個lecture係li dong但係佢真係super fast, assume全世界都識曬咁，上左兩堂就去左sit TK Lam

TK Lam d 手寫notes我覺得比讀ppt果d清晰好多，又會舉例子黎解釋，對於一個教maths既prof我覺得已經係好好

absolute grading 85%↑ a range

做幾多就拎幾多

author: Carl Lin
comment_content:
• Introduction (Ch.1)
• Nash Equilibrium (Chs.2 and 3)
• Mixed Strategy Equilibrium (Ch.4)
• Extensive Games (Chs.5 and 6)
• Repeated Games (Ch. 14)

comment_teaching: The teaching style was engaging and interactive, encouraging students to think critically and participate in discussions. The professor used a variety of teaching methods, including lectures, group activities, and case studies, which kept the content interesting and relevant. They were always available for office hours and provided insightful feedback on assignments. Overall, the teaching was outstanding, and it created a positive learning environment that made the course both enjoyable and educational.
comment_grading: good grading
comment_workload: The workload for this course was reasonable and well-balanced. There were regular assignments, readings, and group projects, but none of these felt overwhelming. The assignments were spaced out evenly across the semester, allowing enough time to complete each one without feeling rushed.

author: Ustspacewoman
title: I love Rong
comment_content:
Just same as other 1013
A little bit easy actually
The class is super easy for mainland students who got a score in math GaoKao around 140
If the foundation of your math is weak, just spend more time asking your classmates and do past paper .  All the types of the questions are the same, just practice.

I would like to say that I love math, and the content is entry for me as a math lover. But according to my discussion with other students, some of them may think it’s difficult. To better understand derivatives and anti derivatives, I strongly suggest a series of videos on YouTube which called the essence of anti derivative. The move of the function is so clear that you can better understand what derivative and anti derivative are.
comment_teaching:
Rong is a really good new teacher. She will upload her note to canvas after class, and it really helps review.
TA Hoi did a really good job.
Actually I learned more in tutorials than in lectures.
I suggest preview before the lecture, going through the modules and read the textbook.
comment_grading:
Hw 10/10  0.1
Mt 94/100 (mean 61 +2SD)  0.35
Final 99/100 (mean 77)  0.55
Overall 97.35/100
Letter grade not released but should be A+
84.75-100 A range
comment_workload:
There are only 10 homework
30min each if you focus on the lecture
If not, don’t worry, just try to first understand the question in the ppt. The examples are typical for a freshman. After that, the homework will be super easy.
All in all, the process of completing the homework is short, but the process of completely understanding the core concepts and different solutions is long.
Plz enjoy your journey in math 1013 if you would choose bba, then it would be your last chance learning math.

author: 日修運動服愛好者
title: Easy to understand
comment_content:
Week


1

Number and intervals, inequalities and absolutes, functions and graphs, composite functions, exponential functions.

2

Trigonometric functions, inverse functions, logarithmic functions, inverse trigonometric functions.

3

Tangent and velocity, the limit of a function, limit laws.

4

Continuity, limits at infinity and horizontal asymptotes, derivatives and rates of change.

5

Basic derivatives, product and quotient rules, derivatives of trigonometric functions.

6

Chain rule, implicit differentiation, derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions and logarithmic functions.

7

Rates of change problems, related rates.

8

Linear approximations and differentials, maximum and minimum values.

9

Mean Value Theorem, derivatives and graphs, L’Hôpital’s rule.

10

Curve sketching, optimization problems, Newton’s Method.

11

Antiderivatives, areas and distances.

12

Definite integrals, the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus.

13

Applications of integrals, the substitution rule.
Some of the parts can be skipped if you have studied related math topic on DSE maths or M2,with using your tricks.
However,the teaching speed is a bit slow...
comment_teaching: Although Mr.Ji comes from Mainland,he is a kind teacher which you will understanding the course even if you got level 3 on M2,you will be satisfied with his teaching that you won't fall asleep in his class
comment_grading:
Really Easy
No mid-term exams
75% Final Exam(online)
25% Online HW
comment_workload: Really low,just finish online homeworks and you can get satisfied grade

author: pepe
comment_content: An easy course, the prof is very nice. I recommend taking this course no matter what. When I get into trouble on my exercise, the tutor are also nice and teach me how to do it.
comment_teaching: Clear presentation of the prof. Lecture notes are very nice too.
comment_grading: I got good score in my midterm. The midterm is quite easy if you understand the concept. You do not need to recite a lot. I think everyone can perform well if you are smart.

author: garbage
comment_content: basic stuff of calculus
comment_teaching:
Cannot tell because I have attended only 2 lessons.
The notes of T K Lam is extremely useful and the textbook is useful as well because T K would select questions from the textbook and you can practice.
comment_grading:
15% homework
35% midterm
50% final
I think the grading would be pretty bad because there are people who have studied calculus in high school studying this course instead of going to MATH 1023. They just break the curve.
mid term: mean: 59.74 SD: 20.96
I did pretty bad in the mid term exam, only got 93/100
it seems that getting an A is pretty hard because you have to be very careful in the exam. The paper checking session is cancelled due to the events this semester and I do not even know where I got wrong. The final is moved to the first week of the Spring Semester.
Update:
The format of the final exam is changed into online canvas quiz due to the coronavirus and it consists of multiple choice questions only. The exam is easier than the past since there is sufficient time and the questions are pretty simple but you really need to be careful because 5 points accounts for each of the question.
mean of the final: 66
I personally got 100 for the final.
Overall I got 97.55 for this course and I got an A+. It is really a good grade and simple course.
comment_workload: Questions in Classviva are pretty dumb. There are 8 assignments in one semester and it takes about 1.5 hours to finish each assignment. You only have to study calculus for like one hour each week. And you would need to mock the past papers given by the professors in order to get an okay grade. The workload is tiny compared to the LANG courses. P.S. I have not learned neither M1 nor M2.
